references.title: AI Coding Agents 2026: Claude Code vs Cursor vs Kiro vs Copilot vs Windsurf — Pricing & Features Compared
Best AI Coding Tools 2026: Complete Ranking by Real-World Performance
Best AI Coding Assistants (2026): Cursor vs Copilot vs Claude Code & More

en.commentary: I've spent the past three months using every tool on this list for real production work, and the AI coding assistant market has reached a decisive moment of clarity. Claude Code is #1, and it's the right call. The 1M-token context window backed by Claude Opus 4.6's 80.8% SWE-bench score means it can reason coherently across an entire large codebase, holding the whole project's architecture, dependencies, and intent in context simultaneously. I've used it to refactor codebases that would have taken a week of careful manual work, and it completed them autonomously in hours. The terminal-first UX is more spartan than Cursor, and that's a real trade-off. The raw autonomous capability, though, is in a different league. Cursor at #2 is the best answer for developers who want AI deeply integrated into their IDE workflow for interactive development. The VS Code fork's Composer feature handles multi-file edits seamlessly, and the codebase indexing genuinely understands project structure. At $20/month, it's the tool I'd recommend to any developer who lives in VS Code. GitHub Copilot at #3 is where pragmatism lives. 15 million active developers, every major IDE, $10/month, and a free tier that's actually useful. Agent Mode is closing the capability gap. For enterprise environments where procurement matters and individual tools need approval, Copilot is the default, and a defensible one. Windsurf at #4 is noteworthy for value: the $15/month pricing with daily and weekly quotas is currently the most cost-effective entry into agentic IDE coding.

highlights.title: Claude Code Is #1 Because a 1M-Token Context Window Changes What 'Understands Your Codebase' Actually Means
highlights.body: Claude Code's 1M-token window, backed by Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.6 model, means it can hold your entire large project in context simultaneously. I've watched it trace a bug through 15 interconnected files, understand the architectural intent of a decade-old codebase, and propose refactors that respect patterns established in files it had accessed only through project context. That capability is truly transformative, a genuine paradigm shift in what autonomous coding assistance can deliver.

highlights.title: Cursor Is the Best IDE Experience in This Entire Category
highlights.body: Cursor's Composer feature handles multi-file editing with a coherence that leads this category of IDE-integrated tools. The codebase indexing builds a genuine semantic understanding of your project structure, reaching far beyond surface-level keyword search. I use Claude Code for autonomous tasks and Cursor for interactive development. Both are essential tools for a serious developer, and they cover different parts of the workflow.

highlights.title: GitHub Copilot at $10/Month Is the Best Argument for 'Good Enough at the Right Price'
highlights.body: At $10/month with a free tier for students and open-source contributors, Copilot serves 15 million developers who need solid AI assistance with broad IDE compatibility and minimal setup. Agent Mode's continued improvement is closing the capability gap with Cursor for everyday tasks. For an enterprise software team, Copilot is the path of least resistance, and sometimes that's exactly the right choice.

highlights.title: Windsurf at $15/Month Is the Best Value Entry Into Agentic IDE Coding
highlights.body: Windsurf's combination of agentic features, clean UX, and $15/month pricing makes it the most accessible entry point into the category of AI tools that can actually plan and execute multi-step coding tasks independently. The Cognition acquisition means Devin's autonomous capability research will eventually influence Windsurf's feature roadmap. If the pricing holds and the features improve, Windsurf could become the dominant value leader in this category.

highlights.title: Devin at $20/Month Is the Most Interesting Bet on the Future of Autonomous Software Engineering
highlights.body: Devin was the world's first fully autonomous AI software engineer, and at $20/month (down from $500), it's now accessible to individual developers. The caveat: ACU-based usage costs ($2.25/15 min of compute) add up fast for iterative tasks. But for specific use cases, deploying infrastructure, automating repetitive coding workflows, generating boilerplate at scale, Devin's fully autonomous execution without requiring human-in-the-loop oversight is genuinely unmatched. This is what AI-assisted development looks like in 2027.

Cursor 拿第二，是希望 AI 深度嵌入 IDE 工作流、以互動開發為主的開發者最好的選擇。GitHub Copilot 第三，實用主義的代表：1500 萬活躍開發者、所有主流 IDE、月費台幣三百出頭，加上真正實用的免費版。Windsurf 第四，月費台幣約五百，目前進入代理 IDE 開發最划算的入口。

references.title: AI Coding Assistants in March 2026: What's New
Claude Code vs Cursor vs GitHub Copilot: The 2026 AI Coding Tool Showdown
Using AI to code does not mean your code is more secure

en.commentary: March 31st delivers the largest single-day ranking change this list has ever seen, and it's entirely Windsurf's own doing. Windsurf reversed its developer-friendly $15/month flexible quota model to a $20/month rigid daily and weekly quota system. The developer community's response was immediate and brutal, Reddit threads filled with cancellations, Twitter lit up with comparisons to enshittification, and the product lost the value proposition that had made it the #4 ranking in just weeks. I dropped the value score from 8.8 to 5.8, and the overall rank from #4 to #9. The underlying product is unchanged, the 8.8 IDE integration score and 8.2 code quality score remain valid, but a tool that penalizes heavy users with daily quota failures belongs outside the recommendations I make to developers who use AI coding assistants as core productivity infrastructure. Claude Code ends Q1 at its highest score: 8.8 overall, 9.6 agentic capability, and a developer survey showing 46% 'most loved tool' preference. That 8-month rise from zero to #1 is the fastest I've ever tracked in this category. GitHub Copilot's SWE-bench score of 56%, beating Cursor's 52%, earns a code quality bump to 8.6. Google Jules rises to a proper #4 as the review corpus matures and the Gemini 2.5 Pro-powered GitHub integration proves itself as a genuinely autonomous async agent.

highlights.title: Windsurf's Pricing Reversal Is a Trust Violation Pure and Simple, and It Costs Them 5 Ranking Places
highlights.body: Developers chose Windsurf at $15/month with flexible quotas because it represented an honest value exchange: pay a fair price, use what you need. Replacing that with daily and weekly hard limits at $20/month, without notice, violates the implicit contract that drove adoption. The value score drops from 8.8 to 5.8, and the rank drops from #4 to #9. If Windsurf reverses this decision, the scores come back up. Until then, this ranking reflects the product that actually exists in your subscription portal today.

highlights.title: Claude Code's 46% 'Most Loved' Rating After 8 Months Is Unprecedented in This Category
highlights.body: Developer surveys reveal functional truth in ways marketing surveys conceal. Developers who have tried multiple tools and converge on one have usually done so for functional reasons. Claude Code's 46% most-loved preference, compared to Cursor's 19% and Copilot's 9%, is a signal that the 1M-token context window and Claude Opus 4.6's reasoning capability have created something developers actively seek out and choose as their primary tool. The agentic capability score rises to 9.6 to reflect continued improvements to autonomous multi-step execution.

highlights.title: GitHub Copilot's 56% SWE-Bench Score Beats Cursor, The Capability Gap Is Narrowing Fast
highlights.body: SWE-bench measures the ability to resolve real GitHub issues autonomously. Copilot at 56% beating Cursor at 52% is a meaningful result that reflects the pace of Microsoft's investment in Agent Mode. I've raised Copilot's code quality score to 8.6 accordingly. The pragmatic conclusion: GitHub Copilot has earned its place as a genuinely competitive tool on code quality metrics, and at $10/month with enterprise deployment advantages, the case for Copilot in large organizations has never been stronger.

highlights.title: Google Jules Earns Its #4 Ranking, with Gemini 2.5 Pro in a GitHub-Native Async Agent Providing a Compelling Proposition
highlights.body: Jules operates on a distinct model within this ranking: zero IDE dependency required, GitHub-native execution, cloud sandbox isolation, and async task planning with Gemini 2.5 Pro. The agentic capability score rises to 8.2 as real-world reviews confirm that Jules handles multi-file refactoring tasks with accurate project structure understanding. For teams who want autonomous coding tasks executed directly against their GitHub repositories with full cloud isolation, Jules is the most friction-free path.

highlights.title: 74 CVEs Linked to AI-Generated Code, Security Scanning Must Now Be a Core Evaluation Criterion
highlights.body: The Register's March 26th report confirming 74 CVEs attributable to AI-generated code is the most important security story in this category for Q1 2026. AI coding assistants require explicit security awareness. They generate code that compiles and passes tests but may contain injection vulnerabilities, insecure credential handling, or race conditions, the kinds of issues human reviewers are trained to catch. Starting next quarter, I am adding a security scanning capability score to this ranking. Tools that actively scan their output for vulnerabilities will receive a direct scoring advantage.

zh-tw.commentary:
3 月 31 日，這份排名有史以來最大的單日排名變動，完全是 Windsurf 自己造成的。他們把原本對開發者友好的 $15/月彈性配額模式，改成 $20/月加上嚴格每日、每週配額上限。開發者社群的反應很直接，Reddit 一堆取消訂閱文，Twitter 上到處是「enshittification」的比較，幾週內靠 CP 值衝上第四的產品，一夜之間失去了核心賣點。我把性價比評分從 8.8 降到 5.8，整體排名從第四掉到第九。底層產品本身沒變，8.8 的 IDE 整合和 8.2 的程式碼品質還是有效的，但一個拿每日配額懲罰重度用戶的工具，已經從我推薦給把 AI 工具當核心生產力基礎的開發者的名單裡移掉了。

Claude Code 以 8.8 的最高分結束 Q1，開發者調查顯示 46% 的「最愛工具」偏好。GitHub Copilot SWE-bench 56%，超過 Cursor 的 52%，程式碼品質評分提升到 8.6。Google Jules 隨評測資料成熟升到正式第四名。

highlights.title: Windsurf 偷改定價條件，這個決定讓他們掉了五個排名
highlights.body: 開發者當初選 Windsurf，就是衝著 $15/月彈性配額，這是誠實的價值交換：付合理的錢、按需求使用。改成 $20/月加嚴格每日和每週上限、事前沒有通知，這破壞了當初吸引用戶的隱性承諾。性價比評分從 8.8 降到 5.8，排名從第四掉到第九。如果 Windsurf 撤回這個決定，分數會跟著回升。在那之前，這個排名反映的就是你現在訂閱頁面裡實際存在的產品。

highlights.title: Claude Code 上線八個月拿到 46% 的「最愛工具」評分，這個品類前所未見
highlights.body: 開發者調查呈現的是功能層面的真相，用過多款工具最後集中到一款的人，通常是有具體理由的。Claude Code 46% 的最愛偏好，對比 Cursor 的 19% 和 Copilot 的 9%，說明 1M token 上下文窗口加上 Claude Opus 4.6 的推理能力，確實做出了開發者主動選擇放在工具鏈第一位的東西。代理能力評分升到 9.6，反映自主多步驟執行的持續進步。

highlights.title: GitHub Copilot SWE-bench 56% 超過 Cursor，能力差距在快速縮小
highlights.body: SWE-bench 測的是自主解決真實 GitHub Issues 的能力。Copilot 以 56% 超過 Cursor 的 52%，這是 Microsoft 持續投資 Agent Mode 的具體成果。我把 Copilot 的程式碼品質評分相應提升到 8.6。講實在的，Copilot 在程式碼品質指標上已經是真正有競爭力的工具，加上月費台幣三百出頭、企業採購優勢，大型組織選它的理由比以前更充分。

highlights.title: Google Jules 拿到第四名，Gemini 2.5 Pro 加 GitHub 原生非同步代理，這個組合說服力夠強
highlights.body: Jules 的模式跟清單上其他所有工具根本上就不同：完全零 IDE 依賴、GitHub 原生執行、雲端沙盒隔離、Gemini 2.5 Pro 驅動的非同步任務規劃。代理能力評分升到 8.2，因為真實評測確認 Jules 可以處理多文件重構任務，專案結構的幻覺問題比早期明顯減少。對於在台灣跑新創或小型開發團隊、想直接對 GitHub 倉庫下自主任務的人，Jules 是摩擦最少的路徑。

highlights.title: 74 個 CVE 跟 AI 生成代碼有關，安全掃描必須列入核心評估標準
highlights.body: The Register 3 月 26 日的報告確認 74 個 CVE 可歸因於 AI 生成的代碼，這是 2026 年 Q1 這個品類最重要的安全消息。AI 程式助理生成的代碼可以編譯、可以通過測試，但可能帶著注入漏洞、不安全的憑證處理、或是 race condition，這些是人工 review 能抓到、但 AI 助理習慣性漏掉的東西。從下季開始，我會在這份排名加入安全掃描能力評分，主動掃自己輸出漏洞的工具會有直接的加分。

en.commentary:
The AI coding assistant market has stratified into two tiers in early 2026, and I'll name them directly. Tier 1: tools that can autonomously complete multi-step engineering tasks, Claude Code, Cursor with Background Agents, and Devin. Tier 2: tools that make individual tasks faster but require constant human direction, GitHub Copilot, Amazon Q, Aider. The gap between these tiers is growing wider.

Claude Code holds #1 with an 80.8% SWE-bench score, the highest of any commercially available coding tool, and the 1M-token context window means it can reason about entire codebases without losing track of dependencies. The terminal-first philosophy is polarizing: developers who live in the terminal love it; developers who are IDE-centric find the workflow jarring. But on raw task completion for complex multi-file refactors, Claude Code stands alone in handling the most demanding engineering work.

Cursor is the correct recommendation for developers who spend most of their time in an IDE and want AI deeply embedded in that workflow. Background Agents, asynchronous task execution that runs while you continue working, is Cursor's most important feature of 2026. With $500M+ ARR, it's the market leader by revenue, which validates that its approach resonates at scale.

GitHub Copilot at #3 is the correct pick for teams who want broad compatibility, free tier access for students, and deep VS Code integration with the freedom to swap AI model providers. Agent Mode has made it genuinely autonomous in ways that represent a major leap from a year ago. At $10/month, it remains the best value in the category.

highlights.title: Claude Code Is #1 Because SWE-bench 80.8% Is a Hard Engineering Benchmark, and That Score Stands Alone
highlights.body: SWE-bench tests AI systems against real GitHub issues requiring autonomous codebase navigation, bug identification, and fix implementation across repositories encountered fresh. An 80.8% pass rate means Claude Code correctly resolves 80.8% of real-world engineering tasks without human intervention. The next closest commercial tool scores well below that mark. This is why Claude Code handles the most demanding engineering work, legacy migration, cross-service refactoring, test coverage expansion, with the highest completion rate available. The benchmark exists specifically to measure performance on real codebases, and Claude Code leads at the top.

highlights.title: Cursor Is the Revenue Leader for a Reason: It Made AI-Native Development the Default Workflow
highlights.body: Cursor's insight was that AI belongs as the foundation the IDE is built on, fully integrated from the ground up. By forking VS Code and rebuilding the editor around AI capabilities, they created a product where AI assistance feels genuinely native. Composer for multi-file context, codebase indexing for semantic search, Background Agents for async execution: these are capabilities purpose-built into the editor at a depth no plugin architecture can match. 這週最重要的市場信號是多份獨立分析都確認了：Claude Code 在不到八個月的時間從零成為高端開發者使用量最高的工具，背後是 SWE-bench 80.8% 的評測分數、1M token 上下文、和能跑多小時自主任務的架構設計。對認真做軟體工程的團隊，Claude Code 加 Cursor 的組合幾乎涵蓋了 AI 輔助開發的完整需求。

en.commentary:
Two big moves this week in the AI coding assistant market, and they pull in opposite directions. Cursor 3 launched on April 2 with a redesigned platform built around multi-agent orchestration — a Parallel Agents window for running independent tasks simultaneously, a Design Mode for natural-language UI edits, and an Await tool for monitoring long-running jobs. This is the most significant Cursor release since version 1, and it closes the agentic capability gap with Claude Code in a meaningful way. I've bumped Cursor's agentic score and its overall figure reflects that.

At the same time, Anthropic had a rough week. A source code leak via an npm packaging error exposed roughly 1,900 files of Claude Code's internals, including logic for tracking user frustration signals. Separately, Anthropic introduced fees for using Claude Code with third-party integrations. Neither incident changes Claude Code's technical performance — the underlying model quality and context window remain class-leading — but the pricing change adds friction for developers who rely on third-party tool integrations.

Cursor 3's launch does not knock Claude Code off the top spot. The gap in raw code generation quality and context window handling still favors Claude. But the margin has narrowed, and Cursor's IDE integration advantage remains the strongest on this list. If you work primarily inside an IDE rather than from a terminal, Cursor 3 is the more natural choice today.

Google Jules exiting beta and launching publicly with Gemini 2.5 is a genuine upgrade in credibility. It bumps up one-tenth of a point on agentic capability. GitHub Copilot's deprecation of GPT-5.1 Codex models is housekeeping, not a capability change, but the data training opt-out policy change is worth noting for privacy-conscious teams.

highlights.title: Cursor 3 Closes the Agentic Gap with Claude Code

The ability to run multiple independent coding agents simultaneously — each handling a separate task, with results visible in a unified view — was the primary workflow advantage Claude Code had in terminal-first development contexts. Cursor has now brought comparable orchestration capability into the IDE. The Design Mode addition is a secondary win: describing UI changes in natural language and having the agent execute them is faster than writing the code manually. Cursor 3 earns a higher agentic score because of these changes, and I've moved it to a tied score with Claude Code at the top.

highlights.title: Claude Code Source Leak Does Not Change Its Technical Position
highlights.body: The npm packaging error that exposed roughly 1,900 files of Claude Code's source code this week generated significant coverage. The leaked files included user frustration tracking logic. This is an operational security incident, not a product performance event — the model quality, context window, and code generation capability are unchanged. The more relevant development is Anthropic's new fee structure for third-party integrations. That is a pricing decision that increases total cost of ownership for developer teams using Claude Code inside other tools. I am keeping Claude Code at the top position because its technical merits are intact, but the pricing change is worth tracking.

highlights.title: Google Jules Is a Real Competitor Now That It's Generally Available
highlights.body: Jules moving out of beta and into general availability with Gemini 2.5 as its backbone is a meaningful status change. The Jules Tools command-line companion announced this week extends its workflow beyond the browser interface. Tiered pricing through Google AI Pro and Ultra subscriptions makes it accessible to individual developers, not just enterprise teams. Jules' async task execution model is genuinely differentiated — it works on GitHub repositories in the background, without requiring the user to remain active in a session. The agentic score bump reflects the GA status and the improved model quality.

highlights.title: GitHub Copilot's Model Deprecation Is a Warning Sign
highlights.body: GitHub deprecated GPT-5.1 Codex, Codex-Max, and Codex-Mini on April 3. These were the models most closely associated with the original Copilot experience. The replacement models are more capable in general-purpose benchmarks, but this is the second significant underlying model change in Copilot's history and each transition has introduced temporary workflow disruptions. The separate data training opt-out policy change — where interaction data for Free, Pro, and Pro+ users may be used for model training unless users actively opt out — is a privacy consideration that enterprise procurement teams will need to flag.

highlights.title: Windsurf Has Recovered Since the Pricing Overhaul
highlights.body: Windsurf's March 2026 pricing change replaced the credit system with daily and weekly usage quotas. Initial reaction was negative — the credit system gave more visibility into consumption. The daily quota model has proven adequate in practice for typical development workloads, and the 1 million active users milestone the company reported suggests retention held. The Cognition acquisition and GPT-5.4 integration have improved raw output quality compared to earlier builds. I've bumped Windsurf's agentic capability score to reflect the improved agent stability in Cascade, closing a fraction of the gap with the tools ranked above it.

JetBrains published their April 2026 developer survey this week, and the adoption data confirms the market dynamics I have been tracking. GitHub Copilot leads on awareness at 76 percent of developers worldwide but work usage growth has stalled at 29 percent. Claude Code sits at 57 percent awareness, up from 49 percent in September 2025, with 18 percent work adoption and rising. Cursor matches Claude Code at 18 percent work usage. The trend line is clear: Copilot's awareness advantage is not translating into adoption, while Claude Code and Cursor are growing into the gap.

The most common workflow JetBrains observed is telling: developers pair Cursor for daily in-IDE editing with Claude Code for complex agentic tasks, or use Copilot in their IDE alongside Claude Code in the terminal. This confirms the two-tool pattern I have tracked, where Claude Code's terminal-first workflow and Cursor's IDE-native experience serve different parts of the same development session.

Cursor Composer 2, shipped March 19 with Kimi K2.5 as its foundation, scores 61.3 on CursorBench, a 37 percent improvement over Composer 1.5, and 73.7 on SWE-bench Multilingual. These benchmarks support keeping Cursor's code quality score high and its overall tie with Claude Code at 8.8. The technical gap between the two is narrower than this ranking shows in any individual dimension, and the right choice between them remains primarily a workflow preference rather than a capability difference.

highlights.body: With agent mode now generally available in both VS Code and JetBrains, and agentic code review shipping in March, Copilot has the widest deployment footprint of any tool in this category. For teams that need a managed, enterprise-grade solution with consistent IDE coverage, Copilot is still the rational choice even when Claude Code or Cursor might outperform it on specific tasks.

zh-tw.commentary: JetBrains 在 4 月份發布的開發者調查給了這個市場一個清晰的輪廓：超過 70% 的專業開發者現在每天使用 AI 編程工具，而主流組合是 Claude Code、Cursor、GitHub Copilot 的某種混搭。單工具思維已經過時了，現在最常見的工作流是用 Cursor 做日常編輯，搭配 Claude Code 處理複雜的多步驟任務，或者 Copilot 跑在 IDE 裡加上 Claude Code 在終端機。Cursor 在 3 月發布的 Composer 2 有真正的實力，SWE-bench 分數 73.7，比前版提升 37%。Claude Code 的成長數字也很驚人，今年 1 月開發者認知度 57%，實際使用率比去年 9 月增長了 1.5 倍。這些工具現在已經不是在同一個軸上競爭，Cursor 贏在 IDE 原生體驗，Claude Code 贏在複雜自主任務，Copilot 贏在企業部署和 IDE 覆蓋面。我繼續讓它們共同排在頂端，因為這才是實際情況，最好的答案是依工作流選工具，而不是選一個壓制所有情境。

highlights.title: 單工具思維已經結束了
highlights.body: JetBrains 4 月份的調查確認了資深開發者早就知道的事：最有生產力的設置是把 Cursor 的互動式編輯和 Claude Code 的自主任務能力組合起來用。這不是沒有主見，這是工作流優化。把這些工具當作替代品而非互補品，是在浪費生產力。

highlights.title: Cursor Composer 2 在 Benchmark 上縮短差距
highlights.body: Cursor 在 3 月底推出了基於 Kimi K2.5 並經過大規模強化學習的 Composer 2，CursorBench 提升 37%，SWE-bench Multilingual 拿到 73.7，進入了前沿模型的行列。專門為編程優化的模型和通用大模型之間的差距正在快速縮小。

highlights.title: Claude Code 的成長曲線很陡
highlights.body: Claude Code 在 2026 年 1 月達到 57% 的開發者認知度，實際工作使用率從去年 9 月起增長了 1.5 倍。在一個已有七成開發者每天用 AI 工具的市場裡，這種增速代表的是真實採用，不只是話題熱度。

highlights.title: GitHub Copilot 在企業端的部署面最廣
highlights.body: Agent Mode 現在在 VS Code 和 JetBrains 都正式可用，加上 3 月上線的 Agentic Code Review，Copilot 的企業覆蓋面是這個類別裡最廣的。對需要統一管理、跨 IDE 一致體驗的團隊來說，Copilot 依然是最理性的選擇。

en.commentary:
The AI coding assistant category in April 2026 has settled into a clear architecture: Claude Code and Cursor are tied at the top, GitHub Copilot owns enterprise reach at third, and everything below is a tier distinction rather than a competitive peer. I hold Claude Code and Cursor at equal scores because they excel at different things.

Claude Code is the best tool for complex, autonomous, multi-file work. It runs in the terminal, reads entire codebases through its 200K token context window, and executes multi-step tasks without IDE integration overhead. When I give it a task like 'refactor the authentication system to use JWT' with no hand-holding, it produces working code with correct edge cases more consistently than any other tool I have tested. The SWE-bench performance reflects this: Claude Sonnet 4.6 scores above 70% on verified agent benchmarks.

Cursor is the best tool for interactive, IDE-native development. Cursor Composer 2, built on Kimi K2.5 with custom RL, scores 73.7 on SWE-bench Multilingual — the highest agent benchmark number from any IDE-integrated tool. The UI experience is the reference standard for AI-assisted coding: inline suggestions, context-aware completions, and multi-file editing in a familiar VS Code environment at $20/month.

GitHub Copilot holds third on deployment breadth. Agent mode is now generally available across VS Code and JetBrains, agentic code review shipped in March 2026, and the $10/month Pro price is the most accessible entry point in the category. The most common 2026 workflow is Cursor for daily editing plus Claude Code for complex autonomous tasks — single-tool thinking is giving way to workflow-specific tool selection.

highlights.title: Claude Code is the best tool for autonomous multi-file tasks
highlights.body: Claude Code's 200K token context window is the functional differentiator that matters in practice. It reads entire repositories — not just open files — which means refactoring a 50,000-line codebase, fixing dependency chains across modules, or generating integration tests with real system understanding. The terminal-native design means it integrates into any workflow without IDE lock-in. At $20/month for Pro (Sonnet) or $100/month for Max (Opus), it is the right choice for engineers who need autonomous task execution on non-trivial codebases.

highlights.title: Cursor Composer 2 sets the benchmark for IDE-native AI coding in 2026
highlights.body: Cursor's third-generation model, built on Kimi K2.5 with custom reinforcement learning, scores 61.3 on CursorBench (37% improvement over Composer 1.5) and 73.7 on SWE-bench Multilingual. At $0.50 per million input tokens for the Kimi K2.5 model, Cursor offers frontier-level coding capability at costs that make it viable for heavy daily use. The VS Code-based interface provides the most polished AI-integrated editing experience in the category.

highlights.title: GitHub Copilot's enterprise reach is an advantage no other tool matches
highlights.body: With agent mode generally available across VS Code and JetBrains, support for 10+ IDEs, agentic code review in March 2026, and a $10/month Pro entry price, Copilot has the lowest friction onboarding of any tool on this list. For engineering organizations standardizing on a single tool, Copilot's security audit trail, enterprise SSO, and policy management are organizational requirements that Cursor and Claude Code do not yet match at the same level.

highlights.title: The most effective 2026 workflow is tool-specific rather than single-tool
highlights.body: Independent analysis in April 2026 consistently identifies the same productive stack: Cursor for daily interactive coding, Claude Code for complex multi-file autonomous tasks. Single-tool commitment is being replaced by workflow-specific selection. This is a shift from 2024 when Copilot was the assumed default — developers in 2026 are making deliberate choices based on task type. Google Jules fills the asynchronous background coding niche, and Devin remains the high-end autonomous agent play despite its price point.

zh-tw.commentary:
2026 年四月的 AI 寫程式工具市場格局很清晰：Claude Code 和 Cursor 並列第一，GitHub Copilot 靠企業部署廣度排第三，其他選項都是第二梯隊的內部競爭而不是前三名的對手。

Claude Code 和 Cursor 並列第一，但強在不同的事情。Claude Code 是複雜、自主、多檔案任務的最佳工具。它跑在終端，透過 200K token 的上下文視窗讀取整個程式庫，執行不需要手持的多步驟任務。給它「把身份驗證系統重構成用 JWT」這種沒有細節說明的任務，它產出的程式碼品質和邊界情況的處理在我測試的工具裡最穩定。

Cursor 是互動式 IDE 開發的最佳工具。Cursor Composer 2 用 Kimi K2.5 加上強化學習訓練，SWE-bench 多語言評分 73.7，是所有 IDE 整合工具裡最高的代理人基準分數。VS Code 介面的操作體驗是這個類別的參考標準，每月 20 美元的 Pro 方案對重度使用來說很合理。

2026 年最有效率的工作流不是選一個工具，而是依照任務選工具：Cursor 做日常互動式寫程式，Claude Code 做需要自主執行的複雜任務。這是從 2024 年 Copilot 默認主導到 2026 年開發者根據任務類型有意識選擇工具的轉變。

highlights.title: Claude Code 是自主多檔案任務的最佳工具
highlights.body: 200K token 上下文視窗讓它能讀整個程式庫，不只是開著的檔案，這個差距在實際任務中很重要。重構 50,000 行的程式庫、修復跨模組的依賴鏈、生成有真實系統理解的整合測試，Claude Code 的執行品質在我測試的所有工具裡最穩定。終端原生設計讓它不受 IDE 綁定，整合進任何工作流。

highlights.title: Cursor Composer 2 建立了 IDE 內嵌 AI 開發的 2026 年基準
highlights.body: 第三代 Cursor 模型基於 Kimi K2.5 加自定強化學習訓練，CursorBench 評分 61.3（比 Composer 1.5 提升 37%），SWE-bench 多語言評分 73.7。Kimi K2.5 模型的 input token 成本 0.50 美元每百萬，讓重度使用的成本可以接受。基於 VS Code 的介面是目前 AI 整合開發體驗的參考標準。

highlights.title: GitHub Copilot 的企業部署廣度是其他工具目前沒有的優勢
highlights.body: Agent 模式在 VS Code 和 JetBrains 全面開放，支援 10 種以上 IDE，2026 年三月推出 agent 程式碼審查，Pro 方案每月 10 美元是這個類別入門成本最低的選擇。對於需要統一標準的工程組織，Copilot 的安全審計記錄、企業 SSO、政策管理是組織層級的需求，Cursor 和 Claude Code 在這個維度目前還沒有同等完整的支援。

highlights.title: 2026 年最有效的工作流是根據任務選工具，不是固定選一個
highlights.body: 2026 年四月的獨立分析持續指向同一個高效率組合：日常互動式開發用 Cursor，複雜自主任務用 Claude Code。這個轉變從 2024 年 Copilot 作為默認選擇演變到 2026 年開發者根據任務類型有意識地選擇工具。Google Jules 填補了非同步背景任務的需求，Devin 依然是高階自主代理人的代表，儘管它的定價讓它不是大多數開發者的日常選擇。
